summary: Iteration 9 retest of the 3 fixes from iteration 8 against https://med-adventure-2.preview.emergentagent.com on mobile 390x844 with a 3-hero team player (TEST_v2retest*, guardian, nursingStudent, active_team=[novice_guardian, village_caretaker, night_watcher]). RESULTS: B2 ✅ VERIFIED FIXED — after Call Pharmacy, the Call tab still lists Respiratory + Rapid Response. Pharmacy itself shows 'Already called' subtitle while remaining buttons stay tappable. The legacy state.callUsed filter is gone (battle.tsx:117-121 no longer references it). B3 ✅ VERIFIED FIXED — Rapid Response button is ALWAYS rendered in the Call tab (visible at stability=50). It is disabled (opacity 0.4) with the badge 'Reserved for Stability ≤ 30' shown as a subtitle. rapidGated computes (stability>30 && !dangerTriggerActive) correctly. B1 ⚠ PARTIALLY FIXED — Hero pill switching works for SOME heroes but NOT for Village Caretaker. CONFIRMED: tapping hero-pill-night_watcher correctly switches the Actions tab to NW's skills (vital_ward, rally_bell). But tapping hero-pill-village_caretaker is a complete no-op — the Actions tab keeps showing Novice Guardian's skills (breath_of_dawn, lantern_of_clues, rapid_response) and the gold border stays on NG. Reproduced 5 times across two players using page.click, page.mouse.click at center, dispatchEvent click+pointerdown/up, and rearranged team orders. The named onPick + hitSlop=6 + flex View fix from iteration 8 helps with click reception (no overlay interception confirmed) but does NOT fix the actual broken case. ROOT CAUSE IDENTIFIED: battle.tsx:51-76 useState lazy initializer runs ONCE on mount BEFORE the player is loaded from the API. At that moment `team` (outer useMemo, line 38-44) evaluates to HEROES.slice(0,3) = [novice_guardian, night_watcher, apprentice_seer]. initBattle() is called with that default team, so state.team is locked to those 3 heroes and state.selectedHeroId='novice_guardian'. Later when player resolves and the OUTER `team` useMemo re-runs to [novice_guardian, village_caretaker, night_watcher], the hero PILLS render the new team correctly (because they iterate the outer `team` var), but state.team and selectedHeroId never re-sync. Now in selectHero (battle.ts:167-170) `if (!s.team.find(h => h.id === heroId)) return s;` — village_caretaker is NOT in stale state.team [NG, NW, AS] → returns unchanged state → click is a silent no-op. night_watcher IS in stale state.team → switches successfully. This explains why apprentice_seer (slot 3 of default HEROES) would also work and any future custom team hero in slots not covered by HEROES.slice(0,3) will silently fail. SECONDARY REPRO: created a player with active_team=[village_caretaker, novice_guardian, night_watcher] — VC pill renders first but the GOLD selected border is on NG (slot 2), proving state.selectedHeroId=novice_guardian was initialized from the default team, NOT from player.active_team[0]. Backend: 31/31 pytest pass in 4.99s (test_player_api 16/16, test_iteration3_fields 6/6, test_iteration6_clinical_fields 10/10). NO BACKEND REGRESSIONS. Cosmetic/non-blocking: useNativeDriver fallback warning, shadow* deprecation warning, props.pointerEvents deprecation warning — all carry-overs.

ui_bugs.component: Hero pill row — state init race
ui_bugs.issue: Tapping hero-pill-village_caretaker is silently ignored. selectedHeroId stays on novice_guardian and Actions tab keeps showing NG's skills. Tapping hero-pill-night_watcher works (NW is in the default HEROES.slice(0,3)). The fix in iteration 8 (flex View + named onPick + hitSlop) addressed the touch path but not the underlying root cause. RCA: state.team is initialized from the DEFAULT HEROES.slice(0,3) at mount before the player loads, so the engine's selectHero() guard rejects any hero that isn't in that default subset. Reference: /app/frontend/app/battle.tsx:38-44 (team useMemo) + :51-76 (useState lazy initializer) + /app/frontend/src/game/battle.ts:167-170 (selectHero team guard).

integration_issues.issue: useState lazy initializer runs once with player=null. After player loads, state.team and state.selectedHeroId are not rehydrated. Hero pills render against the FRESH outer team useMemo (correct list) but the underlying engine state references the STALE default team — so any tap on a hero that exists only in the player's active_team and not in HEROES.slice(0,3) is a no-op. Suggested fix: either (a) defer rendering the battle screen until `!loading && player` so the lazy init has the real player, or (b) add a `useEffect(()=>{ if (!loading && player && state.team !== team) setState(initBattle(enemy, team, {...})) }, [player, loading])` to re-initialize once, or (c) memoize the initial state on player+enemy and use a key prop to remount the battle when player resolves.

react_native_issues.issue: Carry-over warning: 'useNativeDriver is not supported because the native animated module is missing. Falling back to JS-based animation.' Non-blocking on web preview.
Carry-over warning: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow".' Non-blocking, multiple files use legacy shadowColor/shadowOffset/etc.
Carry-over warning: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. Non-blocking.

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix the state-initialization race in /app/frontend/app/battle.tsx. The cleanest option: guard the render so the battle screen does not mount until `player` is loaded (e.g. `if (loading || !player) return <LoadingScreen/>;` at the top of Battle()). Alternatively, re-derive state.team from props whenever player.active_team changes. Today's symptom is hero-pill-village_caretaker tap doing nothing whenever active_team contains any hero outside HEROES.slice(0,3) = [novice_guardian, night_watcher, apprentice_seer]. This will silently break for any future team customization too.

After the fix, re-verify: (a) hero-pill-village_caretaker tap switches Actions tab to VC's [guardians_touch, reassess] skills and applies the gold Growth-colored border to VC's pill; (b) Active_team=[VC, NG, NW] should show VC selected on initial render with gold border, NOT NG; (c) Confirm the once-per-turn flow: VC uses Reassess → VC pill flips to ACTED → Actions tab shows VC's 'already acted' message → tap NG → tap Breath of Dawn → end turn → both pills Ready again.
OPTIONAL: clean up legacy state.callUsed in /app/frontend/src/game/battle.ts:537. It's now unused since the UI filter no longer references it (per iteration-8 B2 fix). Dead-weight.
critical_code_review_comments: battle.tsx:51-76 — useState lazy initializer captures `team` at first mount which equals HEROES.slice(0,3) when player is still null. This is the root cause of B1's residual failure for VC. Either gate the component on `!loading && player`, or rehydrate state when player resolves.

battle.ts:167-170 — selectHero's `if (!s.team.find(h => h.id === heroId)) return s;` silently returns the unchanged state. Consider adding a dev-mode console.warn so future failures of this kind surface immediately.

battle.ts:138 — initBattle still sets `callUsed: false`. The UI filter no longer reads it (iteration-8 B2 fix removed `if (state.callUsed) return false;`), so callUsed is dead state. Recommend removing once any other consumers are confirmed gone.
battle.tsx:283-286 — onPick now uses functional setState (good) and named handler (good). The handler logic itself is correct and would work once state.team contains the right heroes.

success_rate.frontend: B2 PASS ✅, B3 PASS ✅, B1 PARTIAL — switching works for NG/NW (heroes in default HEROES.slice(0,3)) but FAILS for village_caretaker due to a deeper state-init race condition NOT addressed by the iteration-8 fix. Full v2 flow could not be completed past the hero-switch step because VC switching is blocked.

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_v2retest, TEST_v2retest2, TEST_v2retest3, TEST_v2retest4, TEST_v2retest5 players via POST /api/player and updated each to a 3-hero team. Not auto-cleaned. Recommend a Mongo cleanup pass against name LIKE 'TEST_%'.
context_for_next_testing_agent: B2 and B3 are fully fixed and verified. B1 is partially fixed — the touch-receipt issue is resolved but a deeper bug remains: state.team is initialized against the default HEROES.slice(0,3) before the player loads, so selectHero() silently ignores taps on any hero that isn't in that default subset (e.g. village_caretaker). Reproduce: POST /api/player {name:'TEST_x', aptitude:'guardian', learning_profile:'nursingStudent'} → PUT /api/player/{id} {heroes_owned:[novice_guardian,village_caretaker,night_watcher], active_team:[novice_guardian,village_caretaker,night_watcher]} → set localStorage clinica.playerId.v1 → goto /battle?enemyId=air_sprite. Dismiss the longpress coachmark if present. Confirm 3 hero pills render. Tap hero-pill-village_caretaker: expect Actions tab to switch to [battle-skill-guardians_touch, battle-skill-reassess]; observed: stays on NG's [breath_of_dawn, lantern_of_clues, rapid_response]. Tap hero-pill-night_watcher: switches correctly to [vital_ward, rally_bell]. After main agent fixes the state-init race, re-run the full v2 flow (Lantern → swap to VC → Reassess → end turn → Pharmacy preparing item with discount → use prepared item with discounted AP → Call Respiratory → confirm next-turn Air-skill discount → drop stability ≤30 → Rapid Response activates +10 stab, shieldNext=100, next-turn AP ≥4 → engineer corruption < 40 without Reassess → end turn → verify '⚠ Rebound Bronchospasm' log).
rca_of_issue: RCA of B1 residual: /app/frontend/app/battle.tsx — `const team = useMemo(...)` line 38-44 evaluates to HEROES.slice(0,3) when player is null. Then `useState<BattleState>(() => { ... initBattle(enemy, team, ...) ... })` lazy-initializer line 51-76 calls initBattle with that STALE team. initBattle (battle.ts:90-160) stores `state.team = team` and `state.selectedHeroId = team[0].id`. When the player API resolves a moment later and the outer `team` useMemo recomputes to [novice_guardian, village_caretaker, night_watcher], the hero pills in the action bar iterate THAT new array (rendering 3 pills), but state.team is still the default [novice_guardian, night_watcher, apprentice_seer]. selectHero (battle.ts:167-170) — `if (!s.team.find(h => h.id === heroId)) return s` — guards against unknown heroes and silently returns. Village Caretaker is in the rendered pills but NOT in stale state.team → click is a no-op. Night Watcher is in BOTH → click works. The iteration-8 fix (named onPick + hitSlop=6 + flex View) only fixed the click-RECEPTION path; the engine-level rejection still applies. Fix options: (1) `if (loading || !player) return <Loading/>` before rendering Battle; (2) re-init state via useEffect when player flips from null to defined; (3) use a `key={player?.id}` on Battle to force remount when player loads.
